prompt:
In the implementations/controls/ directory of the ieee-research warehouse, create piezo_stepper_eso.py and test_piezo_stepper_eso.py.

Background: Piezoelectric stepper motors use the inverse piezoelectric effect of piezoelectric ceramics to produce tiny step displacements. Core control challenges: hysteresis nonlinearity, creep, temperature drift, external load disturbance. ESO (Extended State Observer) is the core of active disturbance rejection control (ADRC).

## piezo_stepper_eso.py

### 1. PiezoStepperPlant class
- input: voltage u(t)
- state: displacement x1, speed x2
- Built-in simplified hysteresis nonlinearity
- Parameters: stiffness k, damping c, piezoelectric coefficient d
- simulate(u_seq, dt) → displacement sequence

### 2. ExtendedStateObserver class
- Third-order ESO: z1 (displacement estimate), z2 (velocity estimate), z3 (total disturbance estimate)
- Parameter: Observer bandwidth w0
- fal(e, alpha, delta) nonlinear function
- update(y_meas, u) → update status
- get_disturbance() → current disturbance estimate

### 3. ESOController class
- NLSEF control law
- Disturbance compensation: u = u0 - z3/b0
- Tracking differentiator TD
- control(setpoint, y_meas, dt) → control voltage

### 4. ADRController class
- Integrated TD + ESO + NLSEF
- Parameters: wc (control bandwidth), w0 (observer bandwidth), b0 (control gain)
- track(reference, plant, dt) → track tracking

## test_piezo_stepper_eso.py
- ESO convergence test
- Step response test (no overshoot)
- Sine tracking test
- Sudden load immunity test
- Hysteresis compensation comparison

Technical requirements: Python 3.10+, pure NumPy, type annotations, Chinese documentation
Branch name: feat/piezo-stepper-eso
title: Piezoelectric Stepper Motor ESO Control
